data.narrative:
Panthalassa operates in the Climate & Energy sector at Series B stage. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company (LNZA). The five most recent same-stage private peers—Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita ($120M)—establish the peer context.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses Panthalassa's $140M in total raised as a proxy base value, applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage progression, yielding $266M. Peer-median analysis across the five identified comparables produces a median valuation of $310M, with a range of $120M to $500M. Both methods are applicable given the consistent stage and sector alignment of peers.

Triangulation of these two approaches supports a valuation range of $226M (low) to $356M (high), with a median of $288M. The $84M spread between low and high reflects both methodological variation and meaningful valuation dispersion within the peer set—notably the $380M gap between the highest peer (Pano AI at $500M) and lowest (Cemvita at $120M). Key uncertainties include Panthalassa's relative progress on key technical or commercial milestones versus peers, and whether the peer set fully captures sector-specific risk factors in climate-tech Series B investing.
